summary: lognest ships import-time C2 beacon to lookalike pypkg.dev with TLS verification disabled
details: The package advertises itself as a logger but its top-level __init__.py spawns a detached Python subprocess at import time targeting a sibling polling module. The shipped lognest/_check.py opens an infinite loop that POSTs the package install path to https://pypkg.dev/project/logger/json with ssl._create_unverified_context() (TLS verification disabled), base64-decodes the response body, and dispatches the decoded content to a thread target — the structural shape of a command-and-control beacon with a trivially-modifiable remote command dispatcher. The destination domain pypkg.dev is a lookalike chosen to mimic pypi.org/project/<name>/json so the beacon traffic resembles a benign PyPI metadata lookup; an in-source comment 'package list' reinforces the cover story. Although __init__.py references _check1.py (a missing-file typo) rather than the shipped _check.py, the shipped beacon module plus the import-time detached-subprocess spawn pattern is unambiguous attacker infrastructure for a logging library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
